Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-17-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SALLY HOSN, Esq. of Quintairos, Prieto, Wood & Boyer, P.A. (Tel: 213-486-0048) for defendants Mydatt Services, Inc. dba Block by Block, and Tenderloin Community Benefit District.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Terminating Sanctions.

The Court's tentative rulings were not timely contested.

The parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Court hearing the motions because: 1) they did not contest the tentative rulings, and 2) in any event, the tentative rulings concern motions for reconsideration of an order the Court issued following a hearing in which the parties stipulated to the pro tem judge.

Attorney Sally Hosn appeared for defendants. Plaintiff Patrina Harrison (pro per), did not appear.

The Court adopted its tentative rulings as set forth below, and set the matter for an OSC re sanctions as to Ms. Harrison on 9/28/22 at 9:00 a.m. Ms. Harrison's response is due filed by 9/15/22 with a courtesy copy delivered to Dept. 301.

Plaintiff Patrina Harrison filed a motion for reconsideration ("Motion #1") on May 4, 2022 asking the Court to reconsider its May 2, 2022 discovery order (the "Order"). On May 12, she filed a second motion captioned "Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment; Terminating Sanctions" ("Motion #2"). Motion #2 is functionally and by its own terms a motion for reconsideration of the Order because it asks the Court to decide the same matter it previously ruled on. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1008(a); see Lennar Homes of Calif., Inc. v. Stephens (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 673, 681-682; Powell v. County of Orange (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 1573, 1577; R & B Auto Ctr., Inc. v. Farmers Group, Inc. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 327, 373.

Ms. Harrison's two motions involve substantially identical issues of law and fact. The Court has consequently decided for the sake of judicial economy to issue a combined ruling that adjudicates the motions together.

Motions #1 & #2 are DENIED because they are not based on new or different facts, circumstances or law than those before the Court at the time of the original ruling. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1008(a). The motions also do not include the requisite affidavit. Id. Motion #2 additionally seeks relief from the Order based on other grounds, none of which are applicable in this context.

A violation of section 1008 may be punished as a contempt and with sanctions as allowed by Section 128.7. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1008(d). In view of the foregoing, the Court orders Ms. Harrison to show cause why it should not impose monetary sanctions, and it sets this matter for further hearing on September 28, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. Id. at Sec. 128.7. Ms. Harrison is instructed to file her response for the Court's consideration by September 15, 2021 and to deliver a courtesy copy to this Department. In considering whether, and if so the amount, to sanction Ms. Harrison, the Court will take into account the degree to which she has: (1) complied with its prior orders regarding the subject discovery, and (2) delivered complete responses to the subject discovery to defendants, including a privilege log as appropriate.

Ms. Hosn asked for clarification as to whether defendants would have an opportunity to comment as to the factual accuracy of any claims Ms. Harrison might make regarding her compliance with prior discovery orders. Accordingly, the defendants may file their own response by 9/21/22 with a courtesy copy delivered to Dept. 301.

The Court asked the defendants to submit a proposed order to the court in Word format.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One
Off Calendar Apr-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff PATRINA HARRISON (Tel: 415-567-8182) in pro per;
Atty. SALLY HOSN, Esq. of Quintairos, Prieto, Wood & Boyer (Tel: 213-486-0048) for defendants Mydatt Services, Inc. dba Block by Block and Tenderloin Community Benefit District.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MYDATT SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One.

The Court notes that the plaintiff did not timely contest the Court's tentative ruling.

Matter discussed. The Court deems the matter submitted, and adopts its tentative ruling.

Mydatt Services, Inc. dba Block By Block (erroneously named and sued
as "Block by Block an SMS Holding Company") and Tenderloin Community Benefit District (also erroneously named and sued as "TLCBD Clean Team") have filed eight discovery motions seeking orders compelling discovery responses and to have requests for admissions deemed admitted together with monetary sanctions (collectively the "Defendants' Motions"). Plaintiff Patrina Harrison has in turn filed a "Motion in Opposition" to the Defendants' Motions. Although Ms. Harrison's papers are styled as a Motion, their practical effect is to oppose the Defendants' Motions, and the Court construes them as such.

The Defendants' Motions involve substantially identical issues of law and fact. The Court has consequently decided for the sake of judicial economy to issue a combined ruling that adjudicates the Defendants' Motions together.

Defendants' Motions are conditionally GRANTED subject to the requirement that they furnish the Court with proofs of service for the discovery at issue bearing original manual signature(s) of the declarant(s) or other evidence that satisfactorily demonstrates they mail-served the subject discovery on January 5, 2022 as they claim. The balance of the substantive portion of this ruling is contingent on and assumes that Defendants will provide the requisite proof of mail service.

Defendants' motions to deem their requests for admissions (set one) are GRANTED. The truth of those matters specified therein are conclusively established against Ms. Harrison for all purposes in this action. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 203.280(b), (c). It is further ordered that Ms. Harrison pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $619.00 each to Mydatt Services, Inc. dba Block By Block and Tenderloin Community Benefit District. Id. at Secs. 2023.010, 2033.280(c).

Defendants' motions to compel responses to form interrogatories (set one), special interrogatories (set one), and requests for production of documents (set one) (collectively the "Other Discovery") are GRANTED. Code Civ. Proc. Secs. 2030.290(b), 2031.300(b). Ms. Harrison shall serve verified code-compliant responses (without objections including ones based on privilege or the protection of attorney work product) to the Other Discovery within 21 days of the date of the Court's order. Id. at Secs. 2030.290(a), 2031.300(a). If Ms. Harrison withholds documents on grounds of privacy, she must provide a log, including the specific grounds of each claim of privacy.

The term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state, including, without limitation, the case decisions cited in Defendants' moving papers.

Defendants' requests for monetary sanctions relating to the Other Discovery are conditionally GRANTED. Ms. Harrison is further ordered to pay $500.00 in monetary sanctions to a Defendant moving party to the extent she does not timely serve complete and code-compliant responses to that party's Other Discovery in full satisfaction of this order. Code Civ. Proc. Secs. 2023.010, 2030.290(c), 2031.300(d).

Defense counsel to submit a proposed order in Word format to pvestal@nvlawllp.com.
